poem:
**Morning Paper**

In the café of headlines, the strait narrows  
to a throat that could close.  
A minister speaks of land,  
as if land were a second language  
everyone pretends not to know.

Across the page a waiver floats—  
a temporary bridge of oil  
laid on the sea,  
while my spoon circles  
and circles the black coffee.
prompt: You are the poet Billy Collins. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "Middle East crisis live: Iran warns it will close strait of Hormuz if US blockade continues 9.08am BST Turkey’s foreign minister, Hakan Fidan, has told the Antalya Diplomacy Forum that Israel was using security as a pretext to acquire “more land”. “Israel is not after its own security, Israel is after more land,” he said at the annual conference on international diplomacy in the Turkish resort city of Antalya, AFP reported. “Security is being used by the Netanyahu government as an excuse to occupy more land,” he added, referring to Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu. “Israel has to know that the only way to live peacefully in the region … is to let the other countries enjoy their own security, and territorial integrity, and freedom, not to use power on those countries.” Turkey, a Nato member bordering Iran, has positioned itself as a potential key mediator in the Middle East conflict, but its sometimes intense rhetoric against Israel has raised questions over its ability to remain neutral. Updated at 9.09am BST 8.46am BST The Trump administration issued a waiver yesterday permitting countries to buy sanctioned Russian oil and petroleum products at sea for about a month. The move was an extension of an earlier sanctions waiver that expired on 11 April. Following turmoil in energy markets triggered by the Middle East conflict, the Trump administration has attempted to reduce global oil prices by allowing countries to purchase vast quantities of crude oil that had earlier been prohibited under US restrictions.". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
news: Middle East crisis live: Iran warns it will close strait of Hormuz if US blockade continues 9.08am BST Turkey’s foreign minister, Hakan Fidan, has told the Antalya Diplomacy Forum that Israel was using security as a pretext to acquire “more land”. “Israel is not after its own security, Israel is after more land,” he said at the annual conference on international diplomacy in the Turkish resort city of Antalya, AFP reported. “Security is being used by the Netanyahu government as an excuse to occupy more land,” he added, referring to Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu. “Israel has to know that the only way to live peacefully in the region … is to let the other countries enjoy their own security, and territorial integrity, and freedom, not to use power on those countries.” Turkey, a Nato member bordering Iran, has positioned itself as a potential key mediator in the Middle East conflict, but its sometimes intense rhetoric against Israel has raised questions over its ability to remain neutral. Updated at 9.09am BST 8.46am BST The Trump administration issued a waiver yesterday permitting countries to buy sanctioned Russian oil and petroleum products at sea for about a month. The move was an extension of an earlier sanctions waiver that expired on 11 April. Following turmoil in energy markets triggered by the Middle East conflict, the Trump administration has attempted to reduce global oil prices by allowing countries to purchase vast quantities of crude oil that had earlier been prohibited under US restrictions.
